title: Design skill-first Workflow Foundry revamp
summary: Design a complete skill-first revamp of the Workflow Foundry so requests to create skills become explicit callable skill-package contracts, use approved workflow capabilities when needed, and cannot be satisfied by Python, JavaScript, or helper-script substitutes. Trace the current routing and validation failure, define the target architecture and migration plan, and preserve Matt Pocock and ECC phase gates before implementation begins.
acceptance_criteria: grilling maps the complete request path from user intent through project selection, task draft, capability dependency selection, Matt phase work, approved artifacts, validation, review, and handoff
the revamp defines precise foundry terms for a canonical repo skill, a project-local packaged skill, a workflow artifact, and a deterministic helper so scripts cannot be mistaken for skills
task creation and continuation classify the requested deliverable before implementation instead of representing every request as an untyped workflow-change

a task classified as a canonical skill requires a lowercase hyphenated .agents/skills/<slug>/ folder with SKILL.md and agents/openai.yaml; optional scripts, references, and assets remain supporting resources rather than the product
a task classified as a project-local packaged skill names its product boundary and required skill bundle without silently promoting it into the active repo-local .agents/skills surface

Python remains forbidden in this repository; repo-local .mjs remains limited to deterministic JSON validation or query support; existing approved UI or package code stays within its explicit boundary; none of these can satisfy a skill deliverable by themselves
workflow-help exposes an explicit skill-construction route instead of leaving create-skill requests to generic global authoring guidance with conflicting destination roots
capability dependencies influence the required output contract and record provenance; shape-valid dependency metadata alone does not count as a successful skill outcome

validation fails missing SKILL.md, missing or invalid agents/openai.yaml, folder/frontmatter mismatch, script-only skill substitutes, unreferenced helpers, command-first wrappers, and workflow-help discovery drift
eval fixtures cover natural-language create-skill requests and prove the foundry chooses the correct skill surface before any code or helper artifact is approved
the migration preserves the existing task tracker, phase guards, audit separation, read-only tracker UI, source-project isolation, and approval boundary for external writes
the workflow preserves Matt Pocock presentation style and ECC workflow architecture
the workflow produces a reviewable target architecture and migration roadmap before any implementation artifacts are approved

allowed_support_artifacts.purpose: Keep generated skill semantics detailed while the canonical builder remains concise.
Define the one shared deterministic and fresh-agent evidence contract.

required_guidance.evidence: The bundle keeps trigger metadata concise, workflow instructions imperative, and optional resources directly linked.
Shared RED/GREEN scenarios and deterministic release gates verify behavior before completion.

allowed_support_artifacts.purpose: Keep validator and query entrypoints on the same contract and catalog modules.
Exercise contract, catalog, routing, CLI, and read-only projections deterministically.
Register every deterministic data test in the canonical verification command.
Integrate typed contract routing into lifecycle, help, and diagnostic skills.
Report contract and migration violations without mutating them.
Keep the export package install command explicitly classified as package smoke-test support rather than the workflow invocation.

required_guidance.evidence: The modules only validate or query JSON-backed workflow state and do not replace model workflow logic.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Run every English, terse, and Taglish lifecycle, construction, RED, update, helper-pressure, and ownership case in an isolated GPT-5.6 Sol session.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: The 36-case revision was preserved after independent review found missing lifecycle seams, incomplete mixed-product outcomes, and insufficient runner provenance.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: Invalidated release attempt: mixed-product cases did not materialize both primary artifacts, 18 construction-family lifecycle routes lacked live runs, and raw runner event provenance was absent.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Every lifecycle family routes from raw intent, every construction case satisfies every primary deliverable, and every run preserves its raw invocation, events, snapshots, grader output, and attempt history.
behavior_evidence.observed_phase_result: fail: independent review invalidated the 36-case release attempt

validation_results.evidence: Independent Spec and evidence reviews found incomplete mixed-product output, absent live lifecycle routing for construction families, retry laundering, and non-durable runner provenance.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Run every English, terse, and Taglish lifecycle, construction, RED, update, helper-pressure, and ownership case in an isolated GPT-5.6 Sol session.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: Fifty-four clean disposable repositories; exact invocations, raw event logs, before/after snapshots, grader outputs, diffs, and 56 prior attempts are committed with expected routes withheld from every runner.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: 54/54 provenance-complete GPT-5.6 Sol release cases passed in 54 unique sessions; 7 were first attempts, 47 were explicitly linked retries, and 56 prior runs remain preserved without relabeling.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Every lifecycle family routes from raw intent, every construction case satisfies every primary deliverable, and every run preserves its raw invocation, events, snapshots, grader output, and attempt history.

validation_results.evidence: Independent deterministic grading confirmed exact GPT-5.6 Sol invocation, 54 unique sessions, complete attempt chains, raw event logs and hashes, before/after snapshots, boundary-safe diffs or zero-write proofs, valid bundles, mixed-product completeness, and expected raw-intent routes.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Execute all deterministic expanded cases through real contract, readiness, catalog, route, role, artifact, phase, and dependency gates.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: The original fixture selected routes from family_id instead of classifying raw_prompt and did not enforce executable coverage thresholds.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: Invalidated: the fixture materialized family-selected routes and the claimed 80 percent coverage was not an executable threshold gate.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Classify each raw prompt independently, then pass valid products and reject skill substitutes, role confusion, route drift, and boundary violations under real coverage thresholds.
behavior_evidence.observed_phase_result: fail: deterministic route and coverage gate invalidated

validation_results.evidence: The raw prompt never controlled the selected route, and plain node --test emitted no enforceable coverage threshold.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Execute all deterministic expanded cases through real contract, readiness, catalog, route, role, artifact, phase, and dependency gates.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: All 48 expanded cases were classified from raw_prompt independently of family_id and passed real schema, readiness, and catalog paths; schema, readiness, and catalog each exceeded enforced 80/80/80 coverage thresholds.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Classify each raw prompt independently, then pass valid products and reject skill substitutes, role confusion, route drift, and boundary violations under real coverage thresholds.

validation_results.evidence: The Node assertion layer independently classified every raw prompt, compared it to the hidden expected branch, materialized its task and artifacts, and enforced 80 percent line, branch, and function thresholds on schema, readiness, and catalog modules.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Run versioned contract, migration, completion, evidence, routing, bundle, identity, dependency, path, malformed input, and historical-compatibility fixtures.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: Workflow Foundry tracker contract and boundary test suites.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: Contract and boundary suites passed with 80%+ line, branch, and function coverage.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Return stable structured blockers for every invalid contract or boundary and readiness for every valid contract.
behavior_evidence.observed_phase_result: pass: contract runtime and boundary gates

validation_results.evidence: Independent Node assertions covered strict route matrices, nondecorative evidence, identity collisions, bundle parity, external boundaries, and malformed nested state.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Build and verify read-only migration, deliverable, blocker, dependency, and catalog discovery projections.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: Astro tracker data tests, production build, and generated-output test.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: Tracker verification rendered complete read-only state with no mutation controls.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Render typed contract, completion, dependency, and derived catalog state without mutation.

validation_results.evidence: Independent data and generated-output assertions verified rendered readiness, catalog, and absence of mutation controls.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Review workflow-foundry-006 against repository standards, security boundaries, seven issues, and all 44 PRD stories; report only critical or high findings.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: Three independent GPT-5.6 Sol review lanes inspected specification, code/security, and release evidence before final synthesis.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: First review failed with high findings in lifecycle template validity, raw-intent coverage, mixed-product completion, evidence provenance, coverage, symlinks, command detection, legacy identity, traversal, and create/update identity.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Standards and Spec review must find no unresolved critical or high finding.
behavior_evidence.observed_phase_result: fail: high findings required regression fixes

validation_results.evidence: Independent reviewers returned verified high findings; the task remained in code review until each received a regression and a new review.

behavior_evidence.raw_prompt: Re-review workflow-foundry-006 after every high finding is fixed and regression-tested; report only unresolved critical or high findings.
behavior_evidence.repository_context: Independent GPT-5.6 Sol Spec, evidence, and code/security lanes returned PASS; a targeted legacy-identity re-review also returned PASS.
behavior_evidence.raw_output: PASS. Standards and Spec review found no unresolved critical or high finding; all 44 stories and seven issue slices have implementation and evidence traces.
behavior_evidence.expected_route: Standards and Spec review must find no unresolved critical or high finding.

validation_results.evidence: Independent Spec, evidence, code/security, and targeted identity reviewers each returned PASS after verifying the regressions and release artifacts.

session_log.event: Created intake task for a foundry expansion discovery workflow.
Moved to grilling after user asked to grill the foundry expansion discovery workflow.

Resolved operator job: inspect current foundry repo state across projects, skills, validators, trackers, and gaps, then produce a ranked shortlist of candidate workflow additions with evidence, expected value, and the next tracker task to create.

User resumed the task and identified the primary revamp failure: create-skill requests still drift into Python, JavaScript, or helper-script outputs instead of producing actual callable skills governed by the workflow dependency model and ECC.

Completed an evidence-first architecture trace. Root policy is skill-first, but workflow-help has no skill-construction route; initiate-task and continue-task model skill intent as a generic workflow-change; the validator checks existing SKILL.md files but does not require a complete skill bundle, agents/openai.yaml, deliverable-to-output consistency, dependency outcome evidence, or discovery reconciliation.

Reframed workflow-foundry-006 from general expansion discovery into the skill-first foundry revamp owner. Kept the task in grilling with no approved implementation artifacts while the target-surface routing rule remains unresolved.

User instructed the task to continue through PRD, accepting the recommended routing law: foundry/control-plane skills target the canonical repo skill surface, standalone product skills remain in their product repository, ambiguous destinations stop for clarification, and scripts may support but never replace a requested skill. Approved the project-local PRD artifact only; no implementation artifacts were approved.

Closed grilling with a versioned multi-deliverable contract model, three-way skill ownership routing, one dedicated model-invoked skill builder, thin lifecycle delegation, positive bundle and completion enforcement, capability outcome reconciliation, derived skill discovery, and hybrid routing plus disposable-repository regression seams.

Wrote the skill-first Workflow Foundry revamp PRD, moved the task to PRD phase, and set issue breakdown as the next action. No implementation artifacts, dependency calls, commits, or pushes were approved.

Completed a focused PRD review and tightened three contracts before handoff: legacy open tasks are phase-frozen until explicit versioned migration, behavioral eval evidence records raw scenarios and observed outcomes, and $build-workflow-skill is the stable model-invoked authoring authority.

User instructed the task to continue through issue breakdown and explicitly approved pushing the resulting checkpoint. The checkpoint remains scoped to workflow-foundry-006 and must exclude unrelated dirty project and tracker work.

Converted the approved PRD into seven blocker-ordered slices: one validation-core prefactor followed by end-to-end deliverable migration, derived discovery, canonical skill construction, standalone routing, multi-deliverable routing, and capability outcome reconciliation. Every routing slice includes deterministic and fresh-agent evidence, tracker schema writes remain owned by workflow-foundry-004, and no implementation artifact is approved.

Completed focused issue review and resolved all blockers: Issue 002 now requires workflow-foundry-004 tracker authorization and complete contract identity fields; Issue 005 covers both in-foundry packaged and standalone product routes; Issues 004 through 007 share one live-plus-deterministic scenario contract; and command-first regression criteria explicitly cover Quick Start, Getting Started, Example, and unheaded loopholes.

Validated the exact scoped staged snapshot in an isolated worktree. Root workflow-state validation passed; tracker verification passed eight data tests, the Astro production build, and one generated-output test; structural checks confirmed seven issues, all 44 PRD stories, resolvable blocker references, and no implementation artifact approval.

Created scoped checkpoint c3098f8 (docs: plan skill-first foundry revamp) and pushed it to origin/main. The commit contains only the workflow-foundry-006 PRD, seven issue artifacts, task index entry, and task state; unrelated Health, LinkedIn, workflow-foundry-014, and other dirty tracker changes remained outside the checkpoint.

User approved completing workflow-foundry-006 before workflow-foundry-015. Entered implement phase and approved the exact issue-001 characterization test, importable validation core, command wrapper, and test registration paths.

Completed Issues 001 through 007 test-first: importable validation core, versioned role-aware deliverable contracts, explicit open-task migration, derived canonical skill catalog, lifecycle/query/tracker integration, $build-workflow-skill, shared behavior scenarios, GPT-5.6 Sol blind route evaluations, and deterministic rejection/reconciliation fixtures. Readiness reported code-review entry ready.

Closed all first code-review blockers: primary completion gating, strict routing and identity, nondecorative independently graded evidence, command-chain default deny, <=800-line runtime modules, executable 48-case deterministic scenarios, strict packaged/standalone bundle parity, and 36/36 final GPT-5.6 Sol live cases with preserved pre-fix runs.

Completed the final adversarial cycle. Resolved lifecycle-template, raw-intent, mixed-product, attempt-chain, provenance, coverage, symlink, command, traversal, historical-identity, indexed-file identity, and create/update findings; recorded 54/54 provenance-complete GPT-5.6 Sol release cases plus 56 preserved prior runs; independent Spec, evidence, code/security, and targeted identity re-reviews returned PASS.

Closed workflow-foundry-006. Root validation passed; tracker verification passed 55 data tests, independent schema/readiness/catalog coverage gates, the Astro production build, and one generated-output test; the high-severity dependency audit and diff check passed.
